title: Chowmahalla Palace
tagline: Chowmahalla Palace — heritage city of Telangana

overview.short: Chowmahalla Palace or Chowmahallat is the palace of the Nizams of Hyderabad State located in Hyderabad, Telangana, India.
overview.description: Chowmahalla Palace or Chowmahallat is the palace of the Nizams of Hyderabad State located in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. It was the seat of power of the Asaf Jahi dynasty (1720-1948) and was the official residence of the Nizams during their reign. The palace has been converted into a museum and the ownership still lies with the family. The palace was constructed at the location of an earlier palace of the Qutb Shahi dynasty and Asaf Jahi dynasty close to the Charminar.

topPlaces.name: Makkah Masjid, Hyderabad
topPlaces.description: Mecca Masjid, also called Makkah Masjid, is a mosque located in Hyderabad in the state of Telangana, India. It is the largest mosque in the city, and one of the largest in the country, with a capacity of 10,000 worshippers.

topPlaces.name: Jamiat Ul Mominat
topPlaces.description: Jamiat ul Mominat is an Islamic female seminary marriage counseling center, located in Hyderabad, India. It provides education to girls only.

topPlaces.name: Laad Bazaar
topPlaces.description: Laad Bazaar (urdu: لاڈ بازار) or Churi Bazaar is a market located in Hyderabad, India on one of the four main roads that branch out from the Charminar. The bazaar's name, laad means lacquer, which is used to stud artificial diamonds onto bangles.

topPlaces.name: Gunfoundry
topPlaces.description: The Gunfoundry also known as Top ka Sancha (1785 AD) was a cannonball factory set up by the second Nizam of Hyderabad, Nawab Mir Nizam Ali Khan at Fateh Maidan in Hyderabad, India. The historic Aliya High School for Boys and Mehboobia College for Girls, both are opposite to Gunfoundry, across the main road that leads to Abids (in south).

topPlaces.name: Bhagyalakshmi Temple
topPlaces.description: Bhagyalakshmi temple is a shrine dedicated to a Hindu goddess located in Hyderabad, India. This temple is located adjacent to the city's historic monument Charminar.

topPlaces.name: Moghalpura
topPlaces.description: Moghalpura is one of the oldest neighbourhoods in Hyderabad, India, dating back to the Qutub Shahi era. It is part of the old city of Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Charminar
topPlaces.description: The Charminar (lit. 'four minarets') is a monument located in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. Constructed in 1591, the landmark is a symbol of Hyderabad and officially incorporated in the emblem of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Hyderabad
topPlaces.description: Hyderabad is the capital and largest city of the Indian state of Telangana. It occupies 650 km2 (250 sq mi) on the Deccan Plateau along the banks of the Musi River, in the northern part of South India.

items.note: Mecca Masjid, also called Makkah Masjid, is a mosque located in Hyderabad in the state of Telangana, India. It is the largest mosque in the city, and one of the largest in the country, with a capacity of 10,000 worshippers.
Jamiat ul Mominat is an Islamic female seminary marriage counseling center, located in Hyderabad, India. It provides education to girls only.

Laad Bazaar (urdu: لاڈ بازار) or Churi Bazaar is a market located in Hyderabad, India on one of the four main roads that branch out from the Charminar. The bazaar's name, laad means lacquer, which is used to stud artificial diamonds onto bangles.

The Gunfoundry also known as Top ka Sancha (1785 AD) was a cannonball factory set up by the second Nizam of Hyderabad, Nawab Mir Nizam Ali Khan at Fateh Maidan in Hyderabad, India. The historic Aliya High School for Boys and Mehboobia College for Girls, both are opposite to Gunfoundry, across the main road that leads to Abids (in south).
Bhagyalakshmi temple is a shrine dedicated to a Hindu goddess located in Hyderabad, India. This temple is located adjacent to the city's historic monument Charminar.
Moghalpura is one of the oldest neighbourhoods in Hyderabad, India, dating back to the Qutub Shahi era. It is part of the old city of Hyderabad.

The Charminar (lit. 'four minarets') is a monument located in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. Constructed in 1591, the landmark is a symbol of Hyderabad and officially incorporated in the emblem of Telangana.

Hyderabad is the capital and largest city of the Indian state of Telangana. It occupies 650 km2 (250 sq mi) on the Deccan Plateau along the banks of the Musi River, in the northern part of South India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chowmahalla Palace?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chowmahalla Palace is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chowmahalla Palace?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (14 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chowmahalla Palace?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chowmahalla Palace at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chowmahalla Palace?
faq.a: Stays in Chowmahalla Palace start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chowmahalla Palace?
faq.a: The top attractions are Makkah Masjid, Hyderabad, Jamiat Ul Mominat, Laad Bazaar, Gunfoundry, Bhagyalakshmi Temple.
